start.text: Welcome to your reflection space. Let's take a few minutes to look back at today with fresh eyes.

q_energy_check.text: Before we dive in, how would you describe your overall energy as the day closes?

options.text: I feel drained by the day's events.
I feel satisfied with what I've done.
I feel neutral—just another day.

q_locus_friction.text: When you hit a roadblock today, how did it feel to you?

options.text: It felt like the world was pushing against me.
It felt like a puzzle I hadn't solved yet.
I felt stuck because others didn't do their part.

q_locus_impact.text: Looking at your wins today, where did that success primarily come from?

options.text: I got lucky with the circumstances.
My preparation and effort met the moment.
A mix of team effort and my own initiative.

r_locus_victim.text: It seems like today felt like an uphill battle where external factors held the steering wheel.

r_locus_victor.text: You’ve navigated the day with a strong sense of agency. You're seeing the link between your choices and outcomes.

bridge_locus_orientation.text: Now that we’ve looked at how you moved through the day, let's look at where your focus was directed.

q_orientation_focus.text: In your interactions today, which thought feels most familiar to you?

options.text: I hope my hard work was noticed and appreciated.
I hope my contributions helped the team move forward.
I was mostly focused on getting my checklist done.

q_entitlement_check.text: When things didn't go perfectly today, what was your internal reaction?

options.text: I felt frustrated because I wasn't getting the support I deserved.
I wondered why the process is so much harder than it needs to be.
I looked for ways to adjust my own approach to the situation.

q_contribution_check.text: How did it feel when you were able to support a colleague or a project goal?

options.text: It felt rewarding to be the one providing the solution.
It felt like a fair trade for the effort I put in.
It felt natural—that's just how the work gets done.

r_orientation_entitled.text: It's natural to want support, but focusing on what's missing can overshadow the value you bring.

r_orientation_contributor.text: You seem to be deriving satisfaction from the act of giving and solving problems for the collective.

bridge_orientation_radius.text: Finally, let's step back and look at the scope of your impact.

q_radius_scope.text: As you reflect on the most important task you did today, who was it for?

options.text: It was primarily to clear my own backlog or tasks.
It was for a specific user, customer, or teammate.
It was to move the needle on our larger mission.

q_radius_narrow.text: When you finished that task, what was the next thing on your mind?

options.text: What do I have to do next on my list?
How will this affect the person receiving the work?
How does this help my team hit their targets?

q_radius_wide.text: How much of your day was spent considering the needs of people outside your immediate list?

options.text: I was very focused on the people my work touches.
I tried, but my own deadlines took most of my focus.
I prioritized the bigger picture over my personal preferences.

r_radius_self.text: Your focus today was highly centered on your personal execution and immediate responsibilities.

r_radius_altro.text: You've practiced perspective-taking today, looking at your work through the eyes of others.

summary_node.text: Today’s Story: You navigated the day as a {locus}, focusing on your role as a {orientation}, with a {radius} perspective on your impact. Tomorrow is a new chance to expand these boundaries.

end.text: Thank you for reflecting with me. Rest well.
